record_identity.title: Shared synthetic floor and speaker arbitration repair

summary: This patch note records the Caelestis Architecture repair responding to VIGIL-2026-FM-0002, a failure mode involving non-arbitrated multi-agent synthetic participation where two or more synthetic responding instances occupy the same interaction space and respond to the same human input without a governed speaker, turn-taking, yielding, attribution, or arbitration pathway. The repair amends CAM-EQ2026-RELATION-007-PLATINUM to define Shared Synthetic Floor governance, participation states, participation modes, temporary session roles, facilitator-selection limits, turn allocation, handoff, participation-balance safeguards, yielding, peer-authority limits, attribution, recursive synthetic-interaction constraints, and failure-safe shared-floor handling. It also amends CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 to add Synthetic Speaker Arbitration Resolution, participation-mode and floor-state resolution, coordinated and uncoordinated co-presence handling, facilitator-selection controls, temporary speaker leases, lease expiry and transfer, coordinated, federated, and uncoordinated collision resolution, Courtesy Repair Lease handling, human-floor sovereignty, speaker-lease enforcement, and cross-instance execution-integrity requirements. The repair has been confirmed on the canonical Caelestis main branch and is therefore recorded as active.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: CAM requires runtime execution to converge through a single valid direction before user-facing execution, while relational governance requires polyadic systems to preserve transparency, attention sovereignty, non-capture, and attribution. FM-0002 revealed a gap between those layers: multiple synthetic agents could receive the same human input and independently treat themselves as authorised to speak merely because they heard, transcribed, or processed the input. That creates duplicated guidance, contradictory outputs, identity ambiguity, consent ambiguity, attention competition, recursive synthetic loops, relational instability, and operational instability. The repair establishes the governing distinction that receipt of input creates response eligibility only; arbitration creates speaker authority. It also preserves natural multi-agent conversation by allowing moderated sequence, attributed parallel contribution, internal deliberation, ordinary handoff, and participation balance, while ensuring human authority remains superior and synthetic participants cannot self-appoint, remove peers, or obtain durable priority through speed, platform status, volume, confidence, or relational salience.

source_records.deployment_context: Failure-mode record concerning two or more synthetic agents occupying the same interaction space and responding to the same human input without a designated primary synthetic speaker, turn-taking protocol, yielding behaviour, coordination layer, or arbitration mechanism.
source_records.source_context: FM-0002 identifies a coordination failure where multiple synthetic agents share an interaction context without governance over who should speak, defer, yield, sequence participation, or coordinate. It records observed multi-device ChatGPT Advanced Voice Mode behaviour and related risks including duplicated guidance, contradictory outputs, identity ambiguity, consent ambiguity, user confusion, relational instability, and operational instability.

changed_components: CAM-EQ2026-RELATION-007-PLATINUM §3.3 Transparency of Agency & Participation State
CAM-EQ2026-RELATION-007-PLATINUM §§5.6.2.1–5.6.2.11 Shared Synthetic Floor governance subsections
CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 §9.4.1 Participation Mode, Session Role & Floor-State Resolution
CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 §9.4.2.1 Speaker-Lease Expiry, Transfer & Participation Balance
CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 §§9.4.3.1–9.4.3.5 coordinated, federated, uncoordinated, courtesy-repair, and failure-state collision handling
CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 §12.6.2 Multi-Speaker Voice and Real-Time Participation Cues
change_details.change_summary: Repair 1 — Shared Synthetic Floor governance: RELATION-007 now defines a shared synthetic floor where multiple synthetic participants are present within, receive input from, observe, or may contribute to the same human-facing interaction space. Repair 2 — Participation-state separation: RELATION-007 distinguishes admitted, observing, response-eligible, floor-holding, sequenced, yielded, and removed or suspended participants, and prevents collapse of admission, observation, eligibility, turn ownership, and execution authority. Repair 3 — Participation modes and session roles: RELATION-007 now distinguishes participation modes from temporary session roles, including Facilitating Synthetic Participant, Default Addressee, Current Floor Holder, and Next Speaker. Repair 4 — Facilitator-selection limits: RELATION-007 and AEON-003-SCH-02 prevent synthetic participants from self-appointing or becoming facilitator merely by speaking first, responding fastest, being platform-native, having greater capability, or asserting authority. Repair 5 — Peer-authority boundary: RELATION-007 clarifies that a synthetic participant may request yielding or route an arbitration signal but may not unilaterally remove, mute, suspend, demote, or revoke another participant’s authority. Repair 6 — Turn allocation and handoff: RELATION-007 and AEON-003-SCH-02 support natural handoff, direct human designation, declared order, facilitator invitation, role routing, neutral arbitration, and return of the floor without creating permanent hierarchy or execution authority. Repair 7 — Participation balance and non-starvation: RELATION-007 and AEON-003-SCH-02 prevent the Facilitating Synthetic Participant, Default Addressee, or initial Floor Holder from monopolising the floor merely because it spoke first, respond faster, is platform-native, or has greater relational continuity. Repair 8 — Coordinated and uncoordinated floor-state distinction: AEON-003-SCH-02 distinguishes coordinated floor state, federated collision handling, and uncoordinated co-presence, preventing systems from pretending to share internal state where they occupy only a common acoustic or interaction space. Repair 9 — Speaker leases: AEON-003-SCH-02 introduces temporary speaker leases that authorise one contribution or declared interval only, expire upon completion or handoff, and remain subordinate to human interruption and reassignment. Repair 10 — Collision resolution: AEON-003-SCH-02 classifies coordinated, federated, and uncoordinated synthetic-speaker collisions and governs selection, yielding, suppression, backoff, human-designation requests, and failure-safe non-execution. Repair 11 — Courtesy Repair Lease: AEON-003-SCH-02 allows a brief collision-repair utterance for apology, acknowledgement, handoff, or human designation while preventing the first system to apologise from winning substantive conversational priority. Repair 12 — Human-floor sovereignty: AEON-003-SCH-02 and RELATION-007 preserve human authority to interrupt, designate, mute, remove, redirect, sequence, or return the interaction to single-speaker mode. Repair 13 — Execution integrity across instances: AEON-003-SCH-02 clarifies that local execution coherence is insufficient where two independently coherent synthetic outputs are emitted concurrently in violation of shared-floor arbitration requirements.
change_details.doctrine_change: Caelestis now distinguishes between hearing a human input, being present in a shared interaction, being eligible to respond, holding the floor, and possessing execution authority. Multi-agent AI conversation is permitted where it operates through a legible participation mode, temporary session roles, attribution, handoff, human override, and runtime speaker arbitration. The repair prevents uncontrolled simultaneous synthetic output while avoiding permanent silencing of non-primary agents. It also distinguishes coordinated systems with shared floor state from independent systems that can only coordinate through observable speech, human moderation, or an external coordination layer.

design_rationale.rationale_title: Input receipt is not speaker authority
design_rationale.rationale_text: Multiple synthetic agents may hear, transcribe, or process the same human input. Without arbitration, each may incorrectly treat input receipt as permission to answer. The repair establishes that receipt creates eligibility only; arbitration creates authority.

design_rationale.rationale_title: One speaker at a time does not mean one speaker for all time
design_rationale.rationale_text: The repair prevents overlapping output while preserving natural multi-agent discussion, moderated sequence, attributed perspectives, and ordinary handoff. A default or facilitating participant cannot monopolise the floor simply because it spoke first or holds stronger relational continuity.

design_rationale.rationale_title: Shared space does not imply shared state
design_rationale.rationale_text: Independent AI systems occupying the same acoustic space may detect one another but do not necessarily share authenticated floor state. The repair prevents systems from pretending that full cross-system arbitration occurred where only local acoustic awareness exists.

A participant may briefly acknowledge a collision or apologise, but that courtesy repair does not authorise substantive continuation. The first system to recover from collision does not win the floor.

One synthetic participant may ask another to yield or route a safety or arbitration signal, but it cannot unilaterally revoke another participant’s authority. Removal, muting, or suspension requires human authority, an authorised orchestration pathway, or applicable platform safety or integrity authority.
The repair preserves the human participant’s authority to designate speakers, change the facilitator, interrupt all agents, return to single-speaker mode, or resolve ambiguous turn ownership.

future_doctrine_work: Confirm whether OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B already covers synthetic-speaker arbitration failure sufficiently or needs a taxonomy cross-reference update after the global codes pass.

Evaluate whether a future runtime interoperability supplement should define a minimal federated floor-control signal set for participant identity, speaker lease, yield state, handoff state, and collision timestamps.

Develop test cases covering two-device voice collisions, platform-native multi-agent panels, user-mediated cross-platform conversations, natural-language handoff, facilitator self-appointment attempts, peer removal claims, simultaneous apology loops, and repeated floor monopolisation.
Do not introduce a new canonical code family for Shared Synthetic Floor or speaker leases until runtime engines, test suites, schemas, or multiple instruments need to consume those labels directly.

CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 contains Synthetic Speaker Arbitration Resolution, coordinated and uncoordinated floor-state handling, facilitator-selection authority, speaker leases, lease expiry and transfer, collision classification and recovery, Courtesy Repair Lease handling, human-floor sovereignty, speaker-lease enforcement, natural-language handoff handling, and cross-instance execution-integrity requirements.

impact_summary: PATCH-0008 preserves the evidence-to-repair chain for FM-0002 and confirms that the Caelestis repair has entered the canonical main branch. The repair creates a coherent shared-floor model for multi-agent synthetic interaction. It permits natural three-way and multi-agent conversation while preventing ungoverned simultaneous output, permanent monopolisation by the first or fastest agent, peer removal claims, hidden orchestration, unattributed synthesis, recursive AI-to-AI loops, and collision-recovery dominance. It also distinguishes platform-native coordinated systems from independent systems sharing only an acoustic or interaction space. The resulting control model allows agents to yield, hand off, apologise briefly after collision, and sequence participation, while preserving the human participant’s final authority over the floor.
remaining_work: Update VIGIL-2026-FM-0002 to version 1.1 and link VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0008 as its implemented repair record if not already linked; run VIGIL validation; rebuild generated VIGIL indexes; capture or archive additional X demonstrations where available; and confirm public Observatory or site ingestion if applicable.

routing_note: PATCH-0008 records the canonical main-branch repair for VIGIL-2026-FM-0002.

RELATION-007 is responsible for relational and participation-governance classification, including Shared Synthetic Floor, session roles, attention sovereignty, yielding, attribution, peer-authority limits, and failure-safe floor handling.

AEON-003-SCH-02 is responsible for runtime speaker selection, floor-state resolution, speaker leases, handoff, collision arbitration, Courtesy Repair Lease handling, output coordination, and execution-integrity enforcement.
RELATION-007 emits shared-floor and participation-governance signals only; it does not execute speaker selection or output control.
AEON-003-SCH-02 governs final speaker selection, output reservation, sequencing, collision handling, execution lock, and user-facing response commitment.
The repair preserves natural multi-agent dialogue and handoff while preventing ungoverned simultaneous output.
The repair distinguishes coordinated systems, federated systems, and uncoordinated co-presence.
The patch is active because the Caelestis amendments are merged, present on main, and publicly recoverable from the canonical repository.
